channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Around the Electric sub station, 17th July
item.description:
There are a few boxes of small balloon canisters, cans and a double mattress. For health and safety with this current heat I would like these to be removed. Many Thanks
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookhill Close, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Security entrance doors to Fontenelle both broken, 17th July
item.description:
The entrance door system at Fontenelle has been completely broken. Both doors are loose and can open freely to anyone. The call system up to the flats is also out of order.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped stuff, 17th July
item.description:
In park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Franklin Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A dead fox on casino avenue, 17th July
item.description:
The fox is by the road and there’s blood

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 76, Red Post Hill, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: T of Vicarage Grove usual hot spot, 17th July
item.description:
This is local fly tipping Day 1

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vicarage Grove, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: White paint on bus stop, 17th July
item.description:
A paint tray has exploded white paint all over the bus stop. Please could the cleaning team remove the paint splatters. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Northwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin, 17th July
item.description:
Can this bin be emptied more frequently as it’s always over flowing. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Northwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress Flytipped, 17th July
item.description:
Someone has left a mattress on our driveway into the practice by our clinical bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Castleton Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Usual fly tipping. Second time this week alone, 17th July
item.description:
Not much else to say

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vicarage Grove, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked Chute, 17th July
item.description:
Can you please send someone to unblock the chute on 5 th floor Woodsford Portland Street London. Thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Portland Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard knocked over, 17th July
item.description:
I would like to report a bollard that has been knocked over on the footpath directly outside the school. Please could this be inspected and repaired as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Toulmin Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish piling in the street, 17th July
item.description:
The residents from the new development in Wilcox road have not been given bins and they are throwing their rubbish in the street in the Wyvil estate roads and our bins, please see the photo. This is attracting rodents and other vermin. Please can they be given bins and put on the collection schedule and this rivalry rubbish cleared

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Luscombe Way, Vauxhall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Utility works being completed 56 Little Birches, 17th July
item.description:
Continuing works in Little Birches. Why hasn’t the road been closed whilst these works are being carried out. Cars racing up the road and slamming on brakes due to road narrowing by barriers and works being carried out. Noise all night due to company covering works with plastic covering. Constant noise through the day and works starting at 7.30am. Major hazards and danger to public and workers, please investigate asap

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Little Birches, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing timetable frame- damage, 17th July
item.description:
Missing timetable frame- damage

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18, Upper Wickham Lane, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip on Alsike Road, 17th July
item.description:
Various items have been dumped on the verge by Parkway School.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Alsike Road, West Heath, Erith, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dozens of lime and forest bikes, 17th July
item.description:
Dozens of lime and forest bikes blocking the pavement, some cutting off access for wheelchairs/prams
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 445, Woolwich Road, Charlton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste bin pushed over again, 17th July
item.description:
The waste bin at W3E location ///impact.patrol.blur has been pushed over and rolled down the hill again.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Radial Path, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins placed on pavement, 17th July
item.description:
These bins have appeared yesterday on the pavement, they are partially blocking the pavement and they should not be there. They need to be removed immediately, if they have been put there by Lambeth or their contractor then they are in direct violation of their own policy regarding bins and must move them immediately. Lambeth cannot issue fines to residents for bin contraventions and then place bins on the street.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 64, Eylewood Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish not been collected after reporting, 17th July
item.description:
Was told all Rubbish had been picked up, and came home to this! Only a few items been collected. All over the street now, and people dumping on top! I’m guessing it’s now gonna be left all weekend.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evergreen Square, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on allyway, 17th July
item.description:
Path alleyway not usable due to fly tip and bags ripped open . Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Redwing Path, Plumstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
